title: Public awareness and support for the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) implementation in the EU

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on Eurobarometer 447 trend analysis
Estimated: Linear growth trend following initial awareness campaigns
Estimated: Increased media coverage of Cambridge Analytica scandal

events.description: European Commission launches major public awareness campaign for GDPR
Cambridge Analytica scandal breaks, driving public concern for data privacy
Major tech companies begin sending mandatory GDPR compliance update emails

agents.name: European Commission
agents.description: Primary architect and regulator of the GDPR.

agents.name: Věra Jourová
agents.description: EU Commissioner for Justice, Consumers and Gender Equality.

agents.name: Tech Lobby Groups
agents.description: Industry groups concerned about compliance costs and operational friction.

agents.name: Privacy Advocacy NGOs
agents.description: Organizations like EDRi advocating for stronger data rights.

agents.name: General Public (Urban)
agents.description: Tech-savvy citizens generally supportive of privacy protections.

agents.name: General Public (Rural/Skeptical)
agents.description: Citizens concerned about bureaucratic overreach and digital complexity.

notes: Pro-side defined as support for the GDPR framework and increased data protection rights. Against-side defined as opposition due to concerns over compliance burden, complexity, or skepticism of EU regulation. Data quality: Polling trajectory is interpolated based on known start and end points and major exogenous shocks.
